description: 1) Hidden unclean spirit (Matt. 10:1; cf. Rev. 16:13); 2) Indicator of a spiritual maintenance (Lk. 11:24-25); 3) Unbeliever (unclean); 4) Small (Lev. 11:29); 5) Plague; 6) Judgment (cf. 1 Sam. 5:12 & 1 Sam. 6:5 KJV).
notes: & means to combine the scriptures to gain the interpretation. Also see entries under Rat.

description: 1) Ant-eater (destroyer of the diligent) (Prov. 6:6-8); 2) Nosey (as a physical attribute).
1) Needing salvation (Matt. 18:11); 2) Lost (Lk. 15:3-32); 3) In danger of losing salvation (Lk. 9:25; Acts 27:30-31); 4) Ark (Christ) (Gen. 6:13-16; Matt. 24:37-39; cf. Lk. 17:26ff.; Heb. 11:7).
notes: Underlined scriptures display key meaning. cf. = compare scriptures; ff. = and following verses. Also see entries under Adrift, Boat and Sea.

description: 1) Abuse (horrible cold-hearted person) (Exod. 1:22; 7:14).

1) Warning of an attempt to kill the promise (Herod spirit) (Exod. 1:16; Matt. 2:16; Rev. 12:4); 2) Actual abortion.

description: 1) Heaven and earth; 2) Spiritual and earthly (worldly); 3) Heaven and hell; 4) Head and feet; 5) Head and tail; 6) Heaven and the Deep; 7) Victorious and defeated. (1-2) Exod. 20:4; Deut. 4:39; 5:8; 28:23; Josh. 2:11; John 8:23; Acts 2:19; (3) Job 11:8; Ps. 139:8; Amos 9:2; Matt. 11:23; (4) Eph. 1:22; (5) Deut. 28:13; (6) Gen. 49:25; (7) Deut. 28:13.

description: 1) Intimately sharing your heart with another who is not your spouse (Prov. 6:32; Matt. 5:28); 2) Worldliness (James 4:4); 3) Spiritual adultery (worship of other gods) (Jer. 3:8-9, 7:9; Ezek. 16:31-32; Hos. 4:13; Rev. 2:22); 4) Lacking judgment (Prov. 6:32); 5) Snare or trap (Prov. 7:23); 6) Literal adultery (Exod. 20:14; Jer. 29:23); 7) Lust issue (Matt. 5:28); 8) Heart stronghold (Matt. 15:19; 2 Pet. 2:14); 9) Work of the flesh (Gal. 5:19).

1) Holy Spirit; 2) One who comes alongside (“Helper” John 14:16); 3) Jesus Christ (Heb. 7:25, 9:24; 1 John 2:1); 4) Someone who speaks for another/representation; 5) A positive witness.

description: 1) Literal years; 2) Generational association (1 John 2:12-14); 3) Age may also indicate a characteristic portrayed by the number of years (i.e. 15 may portray innocence; 18 may mean complete putting off of the old self; 92 may mean judgment and separation); 4) A woman’s age could be the age of a person or church.
notes: Also see Baby, Grey, Individual Numbers, Old, Young, and Youth.

description: See Before.

1) Waiting for destiny or ministry (Isa. 40:31; Luke 2:25); 2) Transfer of ministries (Acts 15:35-37, 40); 3) Spiritual grounding (Acts 2:42; 2 Tim. 3:16-17); 4) Spiritual refueling (Acts 4:31; 6:3; 7:55; 11:24; 13:2-3, 9); 5) Waiting on the Holy Spirit (Acts 2:1).

This stone which is blue (heavenly) in color relates to the tribe of Issachar (partnership). Therefore this stone means: (1) Heavenly partnership (Exod. 28:19; 39:12; Rev. 21:20); (2) Python or divination spirit (as in the “amethyst python”).
notes: Also see Python and Precious Stones.

description: 1) Hype (false excitement) (Rom. 10:2); 2) Speed (2 Kings 9:20).

As a very general rule, domestic animals may represent: (1) Christ (Lev. 4:3, 14, 23); (2) Believers (Isa. 53:6); (3) Israel. Wild animals may represent: (4) Gentile nations (Jer. 50:17); (5) Unbelievers (Dan. 7:3ff; 8:3, 20); (6) People who return to the ways of the world (2 Pet. 2:12-15; Jude 10-11); (7) Demon powers (Scripturally unclean animals may represent demonic powers).
notes: Also see entries under individual animal names.

description: 1) Walk (Ezek. 47:3); 2) Support; 3) Base/foundation (Acts 3:7); 4) New standing; 5) Preaching the Gospel with strength (Ps. 147:10 & Rom. 10:15; 1 Cor. 4:20); 6) Strained relationship (sprained ankle) (Eph. 4:16); 7) Ineffective preaching (sprained ankle) (1 Sam. 3:19); 8) Unfaithful person (sprained ankle) (Prov. 25:19); 9) Relationship; 10) Broken relationship (broken ankle) (Eph. 4:16).

Appears to have two predominant lines of thought, based on: (1) Humbling and humility; (+) (2) A sign of the cutting away of the flesh; (-) (4) Shame or humiliation; (5) Judgment; (6) Mourning; (7) Fully bald man, like a vulture, could be a demon if unfriendly.
It may be that naturally occurring baldness signifies the former (+), and shaved baldness the latter (-).
